AI Tech Lead Framework · Technical Architecture
Technical architecture briefing

How governed AI-assisted
delivery actually works

The operating model, workflow contracts, engineering convictions, enforcement boundaries, project memory, framework supply chain, and adoption evidence—in one defensible system view.

Runtime

How an engineer's request becomes a planned, verified change.

Governance

Which standards are instructed, inspected, blocked, or proven.

Supply chain

How one source produces three validated, installable distributions.

Core claim: this is a repository-level engineering control plane over existing AI assistants—not another model.

Set the contract: this deck explains mechanics and boundaries, not just benefits. The framework combines probabilistic model behaviour with deterministic local and CI controls.

01Whole system

Three connected systems

Team standardsarchitecture · quality · security
→
Repository control planerules · commands · skills · agents · hooks
→
Delivery runtimeroute · plan · execute · verify · review
→
Evidencetests · gates · audit · project memory

Consumer system

The installed files govern AI-assisted work inside each product repository. CI remains the common deterministic backstop.

request → workflow → guarded writes → verification

Framework production system

Shared source and stack overlays are composed into generated .NET, Angular, and mixed-monorepo distributions.

src → composer → dist → validate → release → install
Do not confuse a written rule with a guarantee. Every control in this deck is labelled by enforcement strength.

The consumer runtime and framework authoring supply chain are deliberately separate. Only generated dist contents ship; the maintainer layer does not.

02Installed anatomy

What lands in a product repository

Instruction

Canonical rules

CLAUDE.md defines the operating contract. AGENTS.md and Copilot material project portable subsets to other tools.

Instruction

Workflows

Commands encode Feature, Fix, Refactor, Test, Review, Design, Debt, adoption, impact, and documentation routines.

Advisory

Skills & agents

Specialised procedures and focused reviewers inspect conventions, SOLID, debt, security, and documentation.

Hard local block

Hooks

Session context, prompt routing, pre-write guards, post-write maintenance, and AI-write audit operate where the client supports them.

Deterministic gate

Scripts & CI

Build, test, architecture, documentation, wiki, impact, and template checks provide executable evidence.

Project memory

Living records

Architecture, ADRs, defaults, learnings, debt, security findings, playbooks, review guidance, and the team wiki.

Diagnostics

Framework doctor

Reports which controls are available on the current machine and AI surface; it does not pretend missing enforcement exists.

Evaluation

Impact harness

Produces capability and scorecard comparisons, with optional repeated same-model behavioural trials.

Generated mirrors are portability mechanisms, not independent sources of truth. Commands are explicit entry points; natural-language routing is a convenience layered on top.

03Request lifecycle

From intent to evidence-backed change

1 · Requestnatural language or explicit command
→
2 · Routeintent contract + security overlay
→
3 · Contextrepo state + architecture + hazards
→
4 · Plan gatescope + approach + verification
5 · Executesmall, verified subtasks
→
6 · Guardinspect writes for defined violations
→
7 · Verifybuild + tests + static checks
→
8 · Review & recordself-review + evidence + knowledge

Explicit command wins

A chosen workflow avoids classifier ambiguity.

Questions are carved out

Answer-only prompts do not inherit unnecessary implementation ceremony.

Security composes

Sensitive work gets extra rails in addition to its primary workflow.

Completion means reporting the command and observed result. “It works” is not acceptable evidence.

Use a concrete example aloud: an authenticated endpoint routes as Feature plus Security. The plan covers API, threat surface, tests, and verification before edits begin.

04Workflow contracts

Intent changes the required behaviour

WorkflowPrimary disciplineRequired evidenceDistinctive constraint
FeaturePlan and incremental deliveryBuild plus relevant testsNo speculative scope; add only required structure
FixReproduce and find root causeFailure before; pass afterDo not mask the symptom or silence the signal
RefactorPreserve observable behaviourBaseline and final verificationNo feature change hidden inside restructuring
TestProve meaningful behaviourDemonstrated defect sensitivityNo tautologies, focused/skipped tests, or implementation mirroring
ReviewRead-only evidence-led assessmentLocated, prioritised findingsNo mutation unless separately authorised
DesignCompare viable approachesTrade-offs and decision recordNo code; uncertainty remains explicit
DebtBounded reduction of known debtVerification plus register updateDo not turn cleanup into an unbounded rewrite
Workflow text instructsGuards block defined patternsTests and CI prove checkable outcomesHumans decide fitness

Workflow rails make the model's process predictable without pretending its output is deterministic. Review and Design are deliberately non-mutating.

05Technical convictions

The engineering position it introduces

Verification over assertion

Run the relevant command, observe the result, disclose uncertainty, and treat failures as information.

Lean delivery

Implement the requirement in front of you. Avoid speculative abstractions, pass-through layers, and ceremonial tests.

SOLID structure

Maintain cohesive responsibilities, stable boundaries, substitutability, focused interfaces, and inverted dependencies.

Boy Scout discipline

Improve touched code when the correction is low-risk and bounded; record larger cleanup rather than smuggling it into scope.

Security by default

No secrets, disabled protections, swallowed failures, or unaudited shortcuts. Sensitive surfaces receive additional review.

Documentation truth

Instructions, architecture, decisions, debt, findings, and learnings are maintained as executable project knowledge.

Leanness and SOLID are complementary. SOLID governs the structure the solution genuinely needs; leanness rejects structure it does not.
Human accountability remains. The framework governs assistance; it does not transfer architectural, security, or release ownership to a model.

These are convictions, not optional style tips. But their enforcement varies: architecture rules can have static backstops; nuanced domain modelling still requires model and human judgement.

06Enforcement model

Six strengths—do not collapse them into “enforced”

1 · Instruction Repository rules and workflow text direct model behaviour; compliance is probabilistic.
2 · Soft context Session and prompt hooks inject branch, architecture, debt, security, wiki, and workflow information.
3 · Advisory analysis Specialist agents and self-review identify design, convention, and maintainability concerns.
4 · Hard local block Pre-write hooks reject defined high-confidence patterns such as secrets, suppressions, and test defeats.
5 · Deterministic gate Build, tests, static analysis, architecture rules, drift checks, and CI fail on machine-checkable violations.
6 · Human authority Engineers remain responsible for correctness, risk, architecture, exceptions, review, and release.
StandardStrongest applicable controlWhat is not proven
Defined secret/suppression/test-defeat patternsHard local block + fixture testsAll possible semantic evasions
Architecture dependency rulesStatic/architecture CI gate where configuredWhether the domain design is good
Mirror and documentation integrityDeterministic drift/dead-instruction gatesWhether prose is strategically complete
SOLID, leanness, Boy Scout judgementInstructions + focused review + selected backstopsUniversal semantic compliance
Business correctnessTests + review + accountable ownerCorrectness outside covered behaviour

The guard is intentionally described as pattern-based. It does not parse every language semantically and must never be marketed as proving that no unsafe code exists.

07Surfaces

Portable rules do not imply identical enforcement

CapabilityClaude CodeCopilot CLI / VS Code agentOther AGENTS.md consumersCI
Portable repository rulesCanonicalMirroredTool-dependentNot applicable
Natural-language workflow routingHook contextClient/version dependentGenerally unavailableNot applicable
Session context preloadNative hookClient/version dependentGenerally unavailableNot applicable
Pre-write blockingExit-code contractJSON deny on supported surfacesNo common contractRe-check what CI can observe
AI-assisted write auditPost-write hookSupported hook surfacesGit history onlyRepository evidence
Build/tests/static gatesCan invoke locallyCan invoke locallyCan invoke locallyCommon deterministic backstop

Different payloads

Claude and Copilot require different output and blocking shapes; the hook twins explicitly dispatch per surface.

Different versions

Older clients can ignore contextual output. The framework doctor reports the actual machine, not an idealised capability.

Common denominator

Committed rules, ordinary scripts, build/test tooling, Git evidence, and CI remain host-agnostic.

PowerShell and Bash twins provide operating-system parity. They do not erase feature differences between assistant products.

08Memory & governance

Every session inherits the team's accumulated context

Architecture

Current structure, dependency direction, hazard areas, and ADRs explain both what exists and why.

Operating defaults

Repository-specific conventions replace repeated prompting and prevent generic assistant defaults taking over.

Delivery memory

Debt, security findings, learnings, playbooks, review guidance, and wiki material preserve unfinished and learned work.

Auditability

AI-assisted file writes are logged where hooks are supported; Git and CI retain change and verification evidence.

Drift controls

Documentation, derived instructions, wiki structure, and executable references are checked against the repository.

Bounded context

Session-start selects high-signal state rather than loading the whole knowledge base into every interaction.

Tech leadowns canonical standards
+
Teamowns context and exceptions
+
CIowns deterministic acceptance
+
Reviewerowns engineering judgement

The framework reduces context loss; it cannot eliminate maintenance. Stale project knowledge becomes confidently wrong model context, hence the explicit ownership and drift checks.

09Framework supply chain

Authored once, composed deterministically, validated three ways

src/coreshared rules and assets
+
src/stacks/*snippets + whole-file overrides
→
Composerstack markers resolved
→
dist/*.NET · Angular · monorepo

Freshness

Rebuild-and-diff proves committed generated output matches canonical source.

Parity

CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md and shipped PowerShell/Bash behaviours remain aligned.

Integrity

Syntax, JSON, markers, encoding, template rules, and stack-specific siblings are checked.

Boundaries

no-meta-leak protects the ship boundary; no-dead-instruction rejects nonexistent commands.

Versioned releasestamp + changelog + rebuild
→
Root installerdetect target stack
→
Reversible adoptionarchive originals; never delete
→
Machine diagnosisreport real enforcement

Only dist contents ship. Maintainer plans, backlog, decisions, build machinery, and internal tracking language stay outside the product boundary.

10Adoption & evidence

Adopt reversibly; decide from observed results

Detect.NET · Angular · mixed
→
Archivepreserve existing material
→
Install & bootstrapproject-specific context
→
Diagnosesurface and machine capability
→
Measurebefore/after evidence

Tier 1 · Deterministic

Capability diff and scorecard show which controls and artifacts are present before and after adoption.

Tier 2 · Behavioural

Same model, same tasks, old versus new framework, repeated trials. Read distributions—not a single lucky run.

Decision

Pilot one active repository for one sprint. Review delivery friction, findings, acceptance, and build evidence before rollout.

Honest boundaries: models remain probabilistic; pattern checks are finite; client capabilities differ; repository rules require maintenance; CI and human review remain authoritative.

Do not invent impact numbers. Run adoption on the target repository and present its report. The value proposition is testable rather than faith-based.

AClaim-to-evidence appendix

Every strong claim has an inspectable mechanism

ClaimImplementationVerification evidence
Natural-language requests receive workflow rails.claude/hooks/route-prompt.*tests/hooks/RoutePrompt.Tests.ps1
Defined unsafe write patterns are rejected.claude/hooks/guard.*Guard.Tests.ps1 + fixtures/guard-cases.ps1
Sessions preload bounded project context.claude/hooks/session-start.*SessionStart*.Tests.ps1
AI-assisted writes are recorded.claude/hooks/audit-trail.*AuditTrail.Tests.ps1
PowerShell and Bash shipped behaviours stay alignedEvery shipped hook/script has twinsTwinParity.Tests.ps1 + cross-OS CI
Generated distributions match canonical sourcescripts/build.*Rebuild then clean git status --porcelain dist/
Maintainer content cannot leak into the productscripts/meta-denylist.txtvalidate-dist check 6: no-meta-leak
Shipped docs cannot instruct nonexistent scriptsDocumentation reference scannervalidate-dist check 7: no-dead-instruction
Installation contract is exercised, not assumedPowerShell/Bash installers; three stack pathsInstallerContract + greenfield/brownfield smoke
Adoption impact can be measured/impact + tests/impact + tests/evalsGenerated capability, scorecard, and behavioural reports
Use this appendix during technical challenge. The implementation and tests are shipped with each distribution so teams can inspect the controls they rely on.

This is a representative evidence map, not an assertion that tests prove semantic correctness. It makes the deck falsifiable: engineers can open the named artifact and inspect the mechanism.
